_comment: Proposed module -> target-package mapping for the langres architecture refactor,
transcribed from the refactor plan's target layout. Input to:
uv run python tools/import_graph.py counterfactual --mapping tools/refactor_target_packages.json
This encodes a NAIVE FILE-MOVE: every module lands whole in its target package.
It deliberately does NOT model the plan's 'contract-first' split of reports.py /
benchmark.py / _report_html.py, because that split was never specified at symbol
granularity -- see docs/research/20260716_import_graph_baseline.md, claim 5.
EXCEPTION, W1: comparator.py WAS split at symbol granularity, so it is no longer a
naive file-move. The Comparator ABC stays in langres.core.comparator -> 'core'; the
concrete StringComparator moved to langres.core.comparators -> 'components', beside
its blockers/matchers/clusterers peers. That single mis-layered module was the ONLY
backwards edge in the components <-> core knot (65 + 1): core/_exports/_blocking
imports the CONTRACT, but the contract's file was mapped to components. Splitting it
also required deleting Comparator.from_schema (the ABC's body constructed the impl --
a second, non-file-level contract -> impl dependency the plan had not accounted for);
callers now use StringComparator.from_schema, which is the identical pre-existing
factory the ABC merely delegated to.
W-1 (PRs #169/#170) added 20 modules the plan's layout had no home for. Mapped here
on the naive-file-move reading -- a facade fragment follows its parent __init__:
langres/_exports/* -> root, core/_exports/* -> core. model_ref keeps its plan-assigned
'core' home at BOTH its new path and the back-compat shim at the old one. These are
transcription decisions, not the plan's: see the doc's post-W-1 delta section.
langres._version (the stdlib-only version leaf that killed the runtime cycle) is
mapped to 'core', NOT to 'root' as the naive-file-move reading would have it. This
is a transcription decision and it is measured, not aesthetic: 'root' re-creates an
architectures <-> root knot (16 mutual pairs all-edges / 10 toplevel) because
core/resolver.py reads the version; 'core' adds no cross-package edge at all (15 / 9
-- identical to leaving it unmapped). A zero-dependency leaf read by three packages
belongs at the bottom layer; putting it in root would re-invert the exact dependency
that PR removed. Change it here if the plan says otherwise.
'exact' wins over 'prefix'; among prefixes the longest match wins.
